headline: China's view on Elon Musk? Visionary, occasional villain
summary: In China, Elon Musk has been the object of love - and occasional loathing.  The Tesla boss has been both lauded as a visionary and criticised by Chinese regulators and the public for the alleged bungling of customer complaints.  In addition, the dominance of SpaceX and its Starlink satellite unit has raised the ire of the People's Liberation Army.

content.title: China's view on Elon Musk? Visionary, occasional villain
content.summary: In China, Elon Musk has been the object of love - and occasional loathing.  The Tesla boss has been both lauded as a visionary and criticised by Chinese regulators and the public for the alleged bungling of customer complaints.  In addition, the dominance of SpaceX and its Starlink satellite unit has raised the ire of the People's Liberation Army.

headline: European Chip Stocks Gain on Foxconn Results, Trump's China Visit
summary: Shares of European semiconductor companies were edging higher after Foxconn reported robust first-quarter revenue and profit and as President Trump brings a delegation of tech CEOs on his trip to China.  Foxconn, which generates a significant share of revenue making AI servers for companies like Nvidia, reported 19% growth in net profit to 49.92 billion New Taiwan dollars and 29% growth in revenue to 2.120 trillion New Taiwan dollars.  Meanwhile, Trump is meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping in a summit attended by Nvidia's Jensen Huang, Apple's Tim Cook and Tesla's Elon Musk.

Meanwhile, Trump is meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping in a summit attended by Nvidia's Jensen Huang, Apple's Tim Cook and Tesla's Elon Musk.

headline: Tesla Stock Rises. China FSD Hopes Are Driving a Rally.
summary: Shares of the electric-vehicle maker rose 0.6% to $448 in premarket trading, while  and  futures were up 0.2% and 0.6%, respectively.  Coming into Thursday trading, Tesla stock has risen five of the past six days, gaining more than 14% over that span.  With CEO Elon Musk traveling with President Donald Trump in China, optimism about Tesla Full Self-Driving (FSD) approval in that country seems to be the main stock driver lately.

headline: Trump’s China Summit Could Mean Big Things for Boeing, Tesla and Nvidia
summary: All eyes are on Beijing this week as President Donald Trump meets with China’s Xi Jinping to discuss everything from soybeans to AI chips.  Markets have muted expectations for U.S. President Donald Trump’s talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping today, but a topsy-turvy geopolitical order is still enough to keep investors on their toes.  The two global superpowers have gathered in Beijing to discuss everything from AI chips to the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Taiwan.

headline: Tesla Stock Wobbles. Full Self-Driving in China Is a Hot Topic.
summary: Tesla  stock was bobbing up and down on Thursday, with investors still focused on China and self-driving technology.  Coming into Thursday trading, Tesla stock has risen five of the past six days, gaining more than 14% over that span.  With CEO Elon Musk traveling with President Donald Trump in China, optimism about Tesla Full Self-Driving (FSD) approval in that country seems to be the main stock driver lately.
